reviewDateNote: Task template supplied reviewDate 'undefined'; actual review date recorded from environment (2026-07-17).
apiVersion: unversioned (no version segment in URLs; no documented versioning or deprecation policy; dated changelog at https://docs.tavily.com/changelog.md; 'fast' and 'ultra-fast' search depths marked BETA as of Dec 2025 changelog entry)

optionalHeaders.X-Project-ID: organize and track usage by project
optionalHeaders.X-Session-Id: opaque identifier for a session of related requests
optionalHeaders.X-Human-Id: opaque identifier for the end-user behind the request
optionalHeaders.X-Tavily-Access-Mode: value 'keyless' enables free, rate-limited keyless access to /search and /extract only (no API key required)

authentication.enterpriseKeyManagement: Enterprise plans can programmatically generate/deactivate scoped keys via a separate host (https://api-key-generator.tavily.com/), authenticated with a Bearer Tavily API key; generated keys have key_type development|production, credit limits (key_limit 1-100,000, default 200) and TTL (ttl_hours 1-8,760, default 10)

endpoints.name: Search
optionalParams: topic (string, default 'general'; options: general, news, finance)
chunks_per_source (integer, default 3, range 1-3, advanced depth only)
country (string; 'prioritize content from the selected country')
auto_parameters (boolean, default false; may auto-select 2-credit advanced search)
exact_match (boolean, default false; only exact quoted-phrase matches)
responseFields: results[] {title, url, content, score, favicon, images, raw_content (if include_raw_content), published_date (topic=news only; format undocumented)}
endpoints.freshness: live web index; FAQ claims 'up-to-date and real-time results'; recency controllable via time_range/start_date/end_date and topic=news

endpoints.name: Extract
requiredParams: urls (string or array of strings; max 20 URLs per request)
optionalParams: query (string; user intent for reranking extracted content chunks)
endpoints.freshness: fetched at request time from the live page (varies by target site)
endpoints.creditCost: 1 credit per 5 successful URLs (basic); 2 credits per 5 successful URLs (advanced); failed extractions not charged

endpoints.name: Crawl
requiredParams: url (string; root URL to begin the crawl)
optionalParams: instructions (string; natural-language crawler instructions, raises cost to 2 credits per 10 pages)
chunks_per_source (integer, default 3, range 1-5; max 500 chars per chunk)
endpoints.creditCost: mapping: 1 credit per 10 successful pages (2 with instructions) plus extraction: 1 credit per 5 basic extractions or 2 per 5 advanced

endpoints.name: Map
requiredParams: url (string; root URL to begin the mapping)
endpoints.creditCost: 1 credit per 10 successful pages; 2 credits per 10 pages with instructions

endpoints.name: Create Research Task
requiredParams: input (string; the research task or question)
optionalParams: stream (boolean, default false; true returns Server-Sent Events)
output_schema (object; JSON Schema for structured output)
endpoints.freshness: runs live web research at request time; asynchronous completion
endpoints.creditCost: dynamic: 4-110 credits (mini) to 15-250 credits (pro) per request, per API Credits doc

endpoints.name: Get Research Task
responseFields: HTTP 200 (completed|failed): request_id, status, response_time, created_at, content (string or object), sources[]
endpoints.pagination: n/a; poll until 200 (polling counts against default rate limits, not the 20 RPM research-creation limit)

endpoints.name: Research (streaming mode)
responseFields: text/event-stream SSE; events: tool call, tool response, content, sources, 'event: done'; each event: id, object ('chat.completion.chunk'), model (mini|pro), created, choices[].delta

endpoints.name: Usage
optionalParams: X-Project-ID (header; scope usage query to a specific project)
endpoints.creditCost: not documented; rate-limited to 10 requests per 10 minutes

endpoints.name: Organization Usage (Enterprise)
requiredParams: organization_name (string, case-sensitive; caller must be org owner)
responseFields: totals {usage, paygo_cost_usd, request_count, breakdown by type}
endpoints.creditCost: not documented; 'Access to this API is an enterprise feature only'; auth uses the organization owner's personal API key

endpoints.name: Deactivate Keys (Enterprise)
optionalParams: request_id (string; deactivates all keys from a previous /generate-keys call)
or Authorization header set to the individual key to deactivate

endpoints.name: Key Info (Enterprise)
requiredParams: not verified in this review (page exists at https://docs.tavily.com/documentation/enterprise/key-info.md; endpoint existence and method confirmed via changelog)
endpoints.source: https://docs.tavily.com/changelog.md (March 2026 entry: 'New endpoints: POST /generate-keys, POST /deactivate-keys, GET /key-info')

coverage.natureOfProvider: Tavily is a web search, content-extraction, site-crawl, and agentic deep-research API optimized for LLMs/AI agents. It is NOT a market-data provider: it serves web pages, news, and web-derived answers, not exchange feeds or instrument-level datasets.
coverage.assetClasses: not applicable / not documented — no instrument-, exchange-, or asset-class-level datasets. Closest financial affordance is the Search 'topic' parameter with a documented 'finance' option (scope of 'finance' topic sources not documented).
coverage.marketsExchangesGeographies: not documented as structured coverage; global open web. Search supports a 'country' parameter to 'prioritize content from the selected country' (prioritization, not a strict filter) and include_domains/exclude_domains for source targeting.
coverage.chains: not applicable — no blockchain/crypto chain datasets documented.
coverage.instrumentTypes: not applicable — no instruments, symbols, or identifiers documented.
coverage.contentTypes: web search results (title, url, content snippet, relevance score), optional LLM-generated answer, raw page content (markdown/text), images, site maps (URL lists), crawled page content, asynchronous multi-source research reports with citations (numbered/MLA/APA/Chicago) and optional JSON-schema structured output.

freshness.search: live web index; vendor FAQ states it 'delivers up-to-date and real-time results' and 'typically returns results within seconds'; recency filterable via time_range/start_date/end_date; topic=news for recent news
freshness.extract: content fetched from target URLs at request time; freshness equals the live page state
freshness.crawlMap: live traversal at request time; timeout 10-150 seconds
freshness.research: asynchronous task (status pending/in_progress -> completed/failed); performs live web research; completion time not guaranteed in docs
freshness.importantCaveat: 'real-time' here means live web retrieval, NOT real-time market data. Nothing from Tavily should be presented as an exchange-grade real-time quote or price feed.

historicalDepth.webContent: no documented index-depth guarantee; Search supports arbitrary start_date/end_date filtering on publish/update date, but the docs do not state how far back the index reaches or how date metadata is derived
historicalDepth.timeSeries: none — Tavily has no historical time-series datasets

pagination.mechanism: none documented on any endpoint. Search is bounded by max_results (0-20); Extract by max 20 URLs per request; Crawl/Map by max_depth/max_breadth/limit; Research is an async task retrieved whole via GET /research/{request_id}.

production.note: Production keys require an active paid plan or PAYGO enabled

rateLimits.enterprise: custom rate limits (pricing page: 'Custom rate limits'); exact numbers not documented
rateLimits.researchPolling: polling GET /research/{request_id} counts against default (standard) limits, not the 20 RPM research-creation limit
rateLimits.onExceed: HTTP 429 with a 'retry-after' header (seconds to wait); message: 'Your request has been blocked due to excessive requests. Please reduce the rate of requests.'
rateLimits.backoffGuidance: docs recommend 'implementing retry logic that respects the retry-after header value'

errors.schema: { "detail": { "error": "<string>" } } — consistent nested detail.error string across documented error responses

statusCodes.400: Bad Request - Your request is invalid.
statusCodes.401: Unauthorized - Your API key is wrong or missing.
statusCodes.429: Too many requests - Rate limit exceeded (includes retry-after header)
statusCodes.432: Key limit or Plan Limit exceeded
statusCodes.500: Internal Server Error - We had a problem with our server.

plans.name: Researcher (Free)
plans.notes: no credit card required; email support

plans.name: Project
plans.price: $30/month (docs api-credits page); pricing page shows slider-based custom amount

plans.name: Enterprise
plans.notes: custom rate limits; uptime and support SLAs; enterprise-grade security/privacy; dedicated account manager and Slack channel (per FAQ)

tierGatedCapabilities.productionApiKeys: require active paid plan or PAYGO enabled
tierGatedCapabilities.keylessAccess: free, rate-limited, no account; /search and /extract only; /crawl, /map, /research require an API key

sdks.methods: search, extract, crawl, map (research via REST)
sdks.maintenanceStatus: official docs page exists; maintenance cadence not documented on docs site
official docs page exists; maintenance cadence not documented on docs site

licensing.redistribution: Prohibited: ToS Section 3.2(ii) forbids customers to 'sublicense, resell, distribute, lease, rent, lend, transfer, assign or otherwise dispose of the Services'; Section 2 makes the API grant 'non-exclusive, revocable... non-transferable, and non-sublicensable' and states the API/keys 'may not be transferred, assigned, shared, or otherwise made available to any third party'.
licensing.storageCaching: Not explicitly addressed for API output in the ToS reviewed; no documented cache-TTL or storage restriction was found. Treat long-term storage/derivative redistribution of retrieved third-party web content as an open legal question (underlying web content carries its own copyright regardless of Tavily's terms).
licensing.attributionDisplay: No attribution or display requirements documented for search results.
licensing.ipOfResults: ToS Section 9.3: Tavily 'owns and will continue to own all rights, title, and interest... in and to the Services'; output ownership is not explicitly defined; customers retain rights to their Input (Section 9.1).
licensing.privacyPosture: FAQ states 'zero data retention' and 'SOC 2 certified'; privacy policy at https://tavily.com/privacy.

limitations: Not a market-data provider: no quotes, OHLCV, fundamentals, reference data, corporate actions, or identifiers. Unsuitable as any pricing/valuation source.
Search result 'content' is snippet/chunk text with a relevance 'score'; result quality and source authority vary — web-derived content requires source vetting before institutional use.
The optional 'answer' field is LLM-generated and must be treated as synthesized (hallucination-risk) content, not a primary source.
No pagination: Search caps at 20 results per request; Extract caps at 20 URLs per request.
'country' parameter prioritizes rather than strictly filters by geography; include_domains on /research is a soft preference, not a hard filter (exclude_domains is a hard blocklist).
Research endpoint credit cost is dynamic and wide-ranged (4-250 credits), making per-report cost hard to budget precisely; it is asynchronous with no documented completion-time guarantee.
'fast' and 'ultra-fast' search depths are marked BETA (Dec 2025 changelog).
No API versioning or deprecation policy is documented; behavior changes are communicated via changelog only — pin behaviors via integration tests.
Historical index depth and how publish/update dates are derived are not documented; date-filtered results may miss or misdate content.
Uptime/support SLAs only at Enterprise tier; usage endpoint is heavily rate-limited (10 requests/10 minutes), limiting real-time quota monitoring.
ToS prohibits resale/redistribution/sublicensing of the Services; storage/caching rights for retrieved content are not explicitly granted.

primaryFor: Qualitative research retrieval: news discovery and monitoring (topic=news with time filters), company/issuer web research, event and controversy scanning
Document/web content ingestion for RAG pipelines (Extract with markdown output; Crawl/Map for IR sites, regulator sites, filings-adjacent web content)
Agentic deep-research report generation with citations and structured JSON output (Research endpoint with output_schema)
fallbackFor: Breaking-news awareness when a licensed newswire is unavailable (with source-quality vetting)
Ad-hoc fact verification and cross-referencing behind licensed data sources
notSuitableFor: Any market data function: real-time or delayed quotes, historical prices, fundamentals, reference/master data, corporate actions, index or benchmark data
Anything requiring redistribution of retrieved content to clients without an independent content-licensing review
hardConstraints: Never present Tavily-derived content as exchange or vendor market data; 'real-time' means live web retrieval only
LLM-generated 'answer' and Research report content must be labeled as synthesized and cite underlying sources
No resale/redistribution/sublicensing of the Service or shared API keys (ToS Sections 2, 3.2)
Production throughput ceiling of 1,000 RPM (standard) / 100 RPM (crawl) / 20 RPM (research creation) below Enterprise
429s must be handled by honoring the retry-after header

free 1,000 credits/month, plan tiers and prices (Project $30/4k, Bootstrap $100/15k, Startup $220/38k, Growth $500/100k, PAYGO $0.008/credit), per-endpoint credit costs, research dynamic pricing 4-250 credits
public plan tiers, PAYGO $0.008/credit, Enterprise custom rate limits + SLAs, student plan
freshness claims ('up-to-date and real-time results'), zero data retention, SOC 2, Enterprise-only SLAs, free tier cap
keyless access mode (X-Tavily-Access-Mode: keyless) for /search and /extract only; crawl/map/research require API key
dated change entries (Dec 2025 - May 2026), BETA status of fast/ultra-fast depths, enterprise key-management endpoints (Mar 2026), absence of formal versioning policy

POST /deactivate-keys behavior (bulk via request_id or single via Authorization header)
POST /org-usage parameters, owner personal-key auth, totals/keys response, Enterprise-only
official Python SDK tavily-python, install command, supported methods
official JS/TS SDK @tavily/core, install command, supported methods
ToS: non-transferable/non-sublicensable API grant, prohibition on sublicense/resell/distribute (Section 3.2), IP ownership (Sections 9.1-9.3), no explicit attribution or result-storage clauses

topic=news 'includes published_date metadata' on search results (2026-07-17 adapter-build recheck); the field's format and exact placement are NOT documented — treat published_date as optional and parse defensively. The /search endpoint reference OpenAPI schema itself does not list published_date.

openQuestions: Exact Enterprise rate limits and SLA terms (documented only as 'custom')

Whether stored/cached API output (retrieved web content) may be retained long-term or embedded in redistributable research products — ToS silent on output storage; requires legal review of underlying-content copyright
GET /key-info request/response details (docs page exists but was not fetched in this review)
Scope and source list of the Search 'finance' topic (not documented)
Historical depth of the search index and provenance of publish/update dates used by date filters
Precise pricing-page Project-tier price (docs api-credits page says $30/month for 4,000 credits; pricing page shows a slider-based custom amount)
PAYGO spending caps/limits behavior behind the 433 'PayGo limit exceeded' status
Live observation 2026-07-17: POST /search with max_results=0 returns HTTP 400 despite the documented 0-20 range — treat 1 as the minimum.
